captions.text: Do they know what caused it?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Disaster officials say their initial assessment points to an electrical short circuit in an air conditioner located in the ceiling.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: And what are police saying about it?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: The national police chief said negligence is the primary theory guiding their investigation right now.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Was there something that made it harder for people to escape?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Possibly. The police chief who visited the scene said access to an exit near the kitchen might have been narrowed by shelving units and lockers.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What's happening with the pub now?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: The pub has said it will cooperate with the investigation into alleged negligence.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw Singapore's court ordered Bloomberg to pay a pretty large sum to government ministers. What happened?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Bloomberg News and one of its reporters were found to have defamed two Singapore government ministers in an article. The high court ordered them to pay about 356,000 dollars in damages.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That's a significant amount. How did Bloomberg respond?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Their editor-in-chief said he was disappointed by the ruling, but that the company stands by its reporter and newsroom. He didn't say whether they plan to appeal.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Did Bloomberg do anything after publishing the article?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: According to an affidavit, a Bloomberg editor said the company lifted the paywall on the article so readers could see a correction notice.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: And what about the ministers' side?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: The law firm representing the ministers didn't immediately respond to requests for comment about the ruling.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I'm seeing news about the U.S. and Iran in the Strait of Hormuz. What's happening there?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: There's a serious standoff. The U.S. has reimposed a maritime blockade of Iranian ports in the strait, and Iran is now threatening to block all oil exporting routes in the region.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That sounds tense. Has there been any actual fighting?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes. There were strikes overnight as the blockade went into effect. Iran responded with retaliatory strikes targeting U.S. military bases in Kuwait, Bahrain, and Jordan.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Why is the U.S. doing this blockade?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Brad Cooper, who commands U.S. Central Command, has accused Iran of attacking seven commercial ships and launching missiles against Gulf countries.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Is this connected to anything else going on in the region?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It's part of broader tensions. There was a ceasefire aimed at ending the civil war between Iran-backed Houthi rebels and a Saudi-led coalition in Yemen.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So this could affect oil shipments?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Potentially, yes. The Strait of Hormuz is a major oil exporting route, which is why Iran's threat to block it is significant.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So Microsoft just put out a huge update for Windows 11?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes, their biggest patch Tuesday ever. They fixed at least 570 security holes — nearly triple what they patched last month.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That's a lot of vulnerabilities. Why so many all at once?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Microsoft says they're using AI now to identify security issues, so they're finding and fixing more problems. They expect future releases to be larger too.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw something about pausing updates — what's that about?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: You can now pause updates for up to 35 days and keep extending that deadline. It gives you more control over when your computer restarts.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Is this part of some bigger effort to improve Windows?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It is. Microsoft's focusing on addressing user complaints and improving performance. Last month they previewed several features that are now arriving in this build.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So Google's about to start carrying rival app stores inside its own Play Store?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes. Starting next Wednesday, July twenty-second, Google says it's ready to let third-party app stores be distributed through Google Play.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: How did we get here? This seems like a big shift.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It comes from a lawsuit with Epic Games that changed how Android app stores work in the United States. Google fought the ruling for a while.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Wait, but Epic and Google were fighting each other, right?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: They were. But they actually settled their legal disputes worldwide and made an eight hundred million dollar deal. Then they tried to undo the original lawsuit ruling together.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: And now they've withdrawn that attempt?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Exactly. They just jointly withdrew it, which means the original ruling stands and Google has to open up.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So could we see something like an Xbox game store on Android now?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: That's the question people are asking. The door is now open for companies like Microsoft to launch their own stores on Android.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: There are protests happening in Ukraine right now?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes, in several cities. People are upset that President Zelensky removed the defence minister.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Who was removed, and why are people so concerned?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: His name is Fedorov. He's only 35 and was appointed just in January, but he's been credited with fighting corruption in the ministry and using data to improve performance at the front.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Has Zelensky said why he made this decision?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Not yet. That silence is part of what's causing the upset among military commentators and parts of civil society.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What else was Fedorov working on?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: His ministry played a significant role in Ukraine's recent attacks on Crimea. Last month, Fedorov said they'd work to cut it off from Russia entirely using mid-range drone strikes.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Is parliament voting on a replacement?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: They were due to vote Thursday on Ihor Klymenko, who currently heads the interior ministry. This is all part of a broader government reshuffle.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: ウクライナで国防相が突然交代させられたって聞いたんですが、何がそんなに問題になってるんですか?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: ゼレンスキー大統領が理由を一切説明していないんです。それが軍や市民の間で大きな反発を招いています。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: 説明なしで交代って、フェドロフさんはそんなに重要な人物だったんですか?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: 今年1月に就任したばかりなんですが、汚職対策やデータで前線の状況を分析する取り組みで高く評価されていました。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: 35歳で就任して半年ちょっとで交代ですか。クリミアへの攻撃とも関係があるって聞きましたが、どういうことですか?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: 彼が率いた国防省は、ロシアが占領するクリミアへの中距離ドローン攻撃に深く関わっていました。

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: フェドロフ氏は先月、ドローン攻撃でクリミアをロシアから「切り離す」とまで宣言していたんです。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: そのタイミングでの更迭となると、後任は誰になるんですか?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: 内務大臣のイホル・クリメンコ氏が後任候補で、木曜日に議会で承認投票が行われる予定です。

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: 同じ改造で首相も替わっていて、国営石油ガス会社トップのセルヒー・コレツキー氏が就任しました。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: これ、ウクライナの戦況にも影響しそうですか?

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So the US military is going to start testing for testosterone levels?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes. Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth announced that military personnel aged 30 and older will be screened for testosterone deficiency as part of their annual health checks.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What's the reason behind this?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Hegseth said it's to make sure troops have the right testosterone levels to operate at their absolute best. He posted about it in a video he captioned 'High-T Department.'

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: And what happens if someone's levels are low?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: They'll be offered voluntary hormone replacement therapy. The screening itself is mandatory, but the treatment would be optional.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Is there a medical reason this matters?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: A doctor who led an FDA panel on this topic last year told the BBC that testosterone is a top marker of a person's overall current and future health. He believes all men over 30 should get screened.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So this applies to all active duty troops over 30?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes, both active duty and reserve personnel aged 30 and older will undergo the mandatory screening during their regular health assessments.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: アメリカ軍がテストステロンの検査を義務にするって聞いたんですが、これってどういう話ですか?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: 国防長官のヘグセスが、30歳以上の軍人全員に毎年の健康診断でテストステロン値を測らせると決めたんです。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: 30歳以上が全員対象なんですね。検査して数値が低かったら、どうなるんですか?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: ホルモン補充療法を受けられます。ただし強制ではなく、本人が希望する場合だけです。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: 軍が率先してやるのは珍しい気がするんですが、なぜ今これが重要視されているんでしょう?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: FDAの専門家委員会を率いたモヒット・ケラ博士が、テストステロン値は今の健康と将来の健康を示す最重要指標だと言っています。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: 軍だけじゃなく、一般の人にも関係してくる話なんでしょうか?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: ケラ博士は30歳を超えた男性は全員検査すべきだとBBCに話していて、軍に限った話ではないという見方をしています。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: これ、今後も動きを追ったほうがいい話ですか?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: ホルモン療法が軍の標準になるかどうか、一般医療にも広がるかどうか、注目しておく価値はあると思います。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw Uganda's asking countries to lift travel restrictions after Ebola. Does that mean the outbreak is over?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Not quite yet. They've discharged their last confirmed patient from hospital, which is a big step. But under World Health Organization guidelines, they need two full 21-day periods without a new case before it's officially declared over.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So why push to lift restrictions now?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Fifteen countries still have partial or full travel restrictions in place. Uganda says those measures have really hurt tourism, trade, and business—even though their response has been relatively successful.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: How bad was this outbreak compared to others we've heard about?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Much smaller. There were 20 confirmed infections and two deaths. Most of those infected were Congolese nationals, plus four health workers and one driver.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Is there still concern about the virus spreading?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Authorities say the threat remains across the border, so they're staying vigilant even as they work to restore normal travel and trade.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: ウガンダがエボラの渡航制限を解除してほしいと訴えているそうですが、今どんな状況なんですか?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: 最後の感染者が病院を退院して、政府が各国に制限解除を求め始めたところです。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: それって、もうエボラは終わったということですか?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: まだ正式な終息宣言は出せていません。WHOのルールで、新たな感染者ゼロの状態で21日間を2回クリアしないといけないんです。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: そうなんですね。それでも制限が続いているのは、実際どのくらいの国が対象なんですか?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: 15か国が今もウガンダへの渡航制限を維持しています。

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: 政府は観光や貿易、ビジネスへの打撃が深刻だと訴えています。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: 今回の流行って、過去のエボラと比べてどうだったんでしょう?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: 死者2人、感染確認20人にとどまっていて、規模は小さかったです。

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: 感染者の内訳はコンゴ国籍者が15人、医療従事者が4人、運転手が1人でした。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: コンゴ国籍者が多いのは気になりますね。国境の向こうのリスクはまだ残っているということですか?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: そうなんです。ウガンダ国内は抑えられていますが、当局は国境を越えた感染の脅威はまだ続いていると見ています。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw something about an HP laptop deal. What makes this one worth paying attention to?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It's a two-in-one convertible with an OLED touchscreen, which is pretty uncommon at this price point. Given that hardware prices are still high right now, it's being called a solid option.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What's special about the screen exactly?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Two things. It's OLED, so you get richer colors and contrast. And it has a sixteen to ten aspect ratio instead of the usual sixteen to nine, which means you can see more content vertically.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Is the resolution decent?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes, it's nineteen twenty by twelve hundred, and it's a touchscreen. For a back-to-school laptop, that's pretty solid.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Why are they making a point about hardware prices?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Storage and memory prices are still at all-time highs, so good laptop deals are rare right now. That's why this discount is getting attention.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: 最近ノートPC、値段が高くて手が出ないんですけど、何かお得な選択肢ってあるんですか?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: HPの2in1ノートPCが今値引きされていて、ちょっと注目されています。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: 2in1って画面を折り返してタブレットにもなるやつですよね、画質とかはどうなんですか?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: OLEDのタッチスクリーンが付いていて、解像度は1920×1200あります。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: OLEDってこの価格帯では珍しいんですか?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: そうなんです、この値段でOLEDはなかなか見かけません。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: 画面の比率が16:10って書いてあったんですが、それって普通と何が違うんですか?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: 縦方向が少し広いので、一画面に表示できる情報が増えます。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: そもそもなんでPC全体の値段が高止まりしてるんですか?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: ストレージやメモリの価格がずっと高い水準のままで、なかなか下がっていないんです。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: じゃあこういう値引きって、今後も期待できるものなんですか?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: 価格が高い時期だからこそ、こういう機会は珍しいと思って見ておいたほうがいいですね。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw something about AI being sold to police departments. What's going on there?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: There's a big push right now to sell artificial intelligence tools to law enforcement. At a recent police technology conference, companies were showcasing AI that can automate routine parts of police work.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What kind of routine work are we talking about?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Things like data gathering and even some decision-making processes. These happen to be critical steps in the legal process.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Decision-making by algorithms? That sounds like a big shift.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It is. The industry is moving toward more automation, even as there's been concern about law enforcement becoming detached from actual human presence in neighborhoods.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Are people worried about this?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes. Public safety groups and legal experts warn that these algorithms often work like black boxes—you can't see how they make decisions. They say this could erode transparency and accountability.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: At a time when trust in police is already an issue.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Exactly. That's why this matters—public trust is already frayed, and these tools could make it harder to understand how policing decisions are being made.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: 警察がAIを積極的に取り入れているって聞いたんですが、具体的にどんな場面で使われているんですか?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: データ集めから判断まで、警察の仕事のかなりの部分をアルゴリズムに任せる動きが広がっています。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: 判断までアルゴリズムに？それって逮捕するかどうかも機械が決めるということですか?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: そこまで踏み込んだ話も出てきていて、今年のIACPという警察署長の技術会議でも自動化が大きな売り文句でした。

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: しかもその自動化の対象が、法的手続きの核心にある作業なんです。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: 法的手続きの核心というのが気になるんですが、それが自動化されると何が問題になるんですか?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: 中身が見えないアルゴリズムが増えると、なぜその判断が下されたかを後から検証できなくなります。

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: 専門家や市民団体は、警察への信頼がすでに揺らいでいる今、透明性がさらに失われると警告しています。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: これって警察側にとっては都合がいいビジネスでもあるんですか?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: 警察の予算はAI企業にとって巨大な市場で、参入する企業が増え続けています。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: 今後この流れは止まりそうにないですか?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: 地域での人間的な警察の存在感が薄れるほど、自動化への依存は強まる傾向があるので、注目し続ける価値はあると思います。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw something about Trump's company selling faster access to Truth Social posts. What's going on there?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Trump Media announced a new service called Truth PSI that would let institutions pay for high-speed access to posts on Truth Social—basically getting them faster than regular users.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So who would want that?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Mainly Wall Street firms. They could use early access to posts from top contributors to make quick trades in stocks, bonds, or interest rates before everyone else sees the information.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Wait—Trump posts on Truth Social all the time. Would this include his posts too?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Possibly. He's the most popular poster on the platform, and as the biggest shareholder in the parent company, he'd benefit directly from this service.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That seems like a conflict of interest.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Critics are calling it brazen corruption—saying it exploits the presidency for profit, especially since his posts can affect national security and financial markets.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Don't other platforms do something similar though?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes, rival platforms have offered paid access. But the key difference here is that the president himself is both the top poster and a major shareholder who profits from the service.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: Truth Social が、投稿への優先アクセス権を有料で販売する計画を発表しました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: サービスの名称は「Truth PSI」で、木曜日に明らかになりました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ウォール街の取引会社などが、主要な投稿者の情報をいち早く受け取れる仕組みです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 株式や債券、金利の動きを先読みして利益を得ることが目的とみられています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: Truth Social で最も影響力のある投稿者は、現職大統領のトランプ氏本人です。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: トランプ氏は上場親会社の筆頭株主でもあり、このサービスから直接利益を得る立場にあります。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 批判派はこれを、大統領の地位を私的な利益のために使う行為だと強く非難しています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 国家安全保障や金融市場への影響を懸念する声も、すでに上がっています。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So Britain just nationalised a steel company? That seems like a big move.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes, British Steel was brought under public ownership on Thursday. The government said it's to protect the future of steel production.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Why did they feel they needed to step in?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: About 15 months ago, the government intervened to prevent the closure of the steelworks in Scunthorpe and the loss of 4,000 jobs. Now they say nationalisation is essential to maintain production there and protect UK supply chains.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: And China's upset about this?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: China's government says it's strongly dissatisfied. British Steel was previously owned by a Chinese company called Jingye.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Is this part of a pattern with Chinese ownership?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It does follow other moves. Last year, the government backed Britain's biggest nuclear project, Sizewell C, after easing out China General Nuclear, which had held a 20% stake. That was over security fears back in 2021.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So there's a broader tension around Chinese investment in critical infrastructure.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Exactly. Steel production and nuclear energy are both seen as strategically important, and the government appears to be taking a more cautious approach.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 英国の製鉄会社ブリティッシュ・スチールが、今週公的管理下に入りました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: これに対し、中国政府は強い不満を示す声明を出しています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 国有化の背景には、リンカンシャー州スカンソープの製鉄所を守る必要がありました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 操業が止まれば、約4,000人の雇用が失われる恐れがあったのです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ブリティッシュ・スチールはもともと、中国の京業（ジンイェ）が所有していました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 英国政府にとって、鉄鋼の国内生産とサプライチェーンの維持は重要な課題です。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 英中関係という点では、こうした動きは今に始まったことではありません。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 2021年には安全保障上の懸念から、原発プロジェクトへの中国広核集団の出資が外されています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 昨年には、そのサイズウェルCに対して380億ポンドの支援が決まりました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 英国が自国の基幹産業や重要インフラをどう守るか、その姿勢が改めて問われています。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw Trump made some claims about China and elections. What's this about?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: In a televised address, Trump accused China of interfering with the 2020 election. He announced he was declassifying intelligence about what he called vulnerabilities in election infrastructure.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Is there evidence backing this up?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: The headline describes his claims as unverified. Democratic Senator Mark Warner, who's vice-chair of the Senate intelligence committee, has worked on election security and acknowledges China as a serious competitor, but he didn't confirm Trump's specific allegations.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Why are people concerned about this now?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Democrats are warning that Trump may be trying to lay groundwork to challenge the upcoming midterm election results. Polls suggest his party could face significant losses.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So they think it's more about the midterms than 2020?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: That's the concern, yes. Critics say he may be spreading confusion and misinformation as a way to contest results if they don't go his way.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: トランプ前大統領が、2020年大統領選に中国が介入したと主張する演説を行いました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: プライムタイムのテレビ放送という場を選んだことで、この発言は広く注目を集めています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 演説の中でトランプ氏は、選挙インフラに関わる機密情報を即時に公開すると宣言しました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ただし、中国の介入を裏付ける具体的な証拠は、現時点では示されていません。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 民主党側は、この主張が来たる中間選挙の結果に異議を唱えるための準備だと見ています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 上院情報委員会副委員長のマーク・ワーナー議員は、外国による選挙介入への対策に長年携わってきた人物です。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 同議員は、中国が米国の利益を犠牲にして自国の目標を追う姿勢は明らかだと述べています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 世論調査では、中間選挙で共和党が苦しい結果を迎える可能性が示されています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 混乱を意図的に広げることで、選挙結果への不信感を植え付けようとしているという懸念は根強くあります。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So the EU just told Google to open up Android to other AI companies. That sounds like bad news for Google, no?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: On the surface, yes. But Google actually got something really valuable: time. They have until July 2027 to make those changes.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: And that matters because...?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It gives them roughly a year to keep expanding Gemini without competition, and to help shape exactly how rivals will plug into Android down the road.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Wait, how does this compare to what Apple has to do?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Apple has to make Siri compliant with EU rules before launch. No grace period. So Google may have played the regulatory game more shrewdly.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Played it how?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: By engaging with Brussels early and strategically. What looks like a defeat on paper is actually a win — they resisted this kind of access for years, and now they get to ease into it on their own timeline.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: EUがGoogleに対し、AndroidをAI競合他社へ開放するよう正式に命じました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 世界で数十億台の端末を支えるAndroidが、対象となっています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ただしGoogleには、対応期限として2027年7月までの猶予が与えられました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: この約一年の間に、GeminiをさらにAndroidへ根付かせることができます。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: EUとの技術的な交渉も、Googleが主導する形で進められる見通しです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 一方のAppleは、Siriの新しいAI機能をリリース前に規制へ準拠させる必要があります。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 表面上はGoogleへの制裁に見えますが、実態は有利な条件を引き出した結果です。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 規制の場でどう立ち回るかが、企業の競争力を左右する時代になっています。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So New York just paused new AI data centers, but the governor is using AI herself?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Right. Governor Kathy Hochul says her team is using AI to review every rule and regulation in the state to find outdated laws.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What kind of outdated laws are we talking about?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Things like a twenty-five dollar fee to take a dog hunting, or requiring pregnant people to get a permit to work after midnight.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That's pretty specific. Why did they pause the data centers then?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: New York became the first state to put a hold on new hyperscale data centers for up to a year. Lawmakers are concerned about rising utility costs and threats to natural resources.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So they're trying to figure out regulations while the pause is in effect?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Exactly. They want to protect residents before allowing more massive data centers to be built.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ニューヨーク州のキャシー・ホークル知事が、AIを使って州の法規制を丸ごと見直す取り組みを進めています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ブルームバーグのポッドキャストで知事は、古くなった規則を洗い出すためにAIを活用していると話しました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: その中には、猟犬を連れて狩りをする際に25ドルの費用がかかるという規定も含まれています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 妊娠中の人が深夜以降に働くには許可が必要、という条項も残っているそうです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 一方でホークル知事は今週、大規模なデータセンターの新設を最長一年間停止する措置にも署名しました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: これは全米で初めての対応で、光熱費の上昇や自然環境への負荷を懸念する声が背景にあります。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 州議会は今後、住民を守るための新たな規制の整備を進める方針です。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 法律を見直す道具としてAIを使いながら、AIそのものの影響には慎重に向き合う、そんな姿勢がこの動きには表れています。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw something about police taking an activist to hospital in India. What happened?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: A prominent activist was forcibly removed from a protest site in New Delhi and hospitalized. He'd been on a hunger strike for twenty days.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What was he protesting?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Alleged corruption in India's exam system. It's become a major issue there.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: How serious is this exam corruption?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Very. About two point two million aspiring doctors had to retake the medical entrance exam last month after questions from the original May test were leaked.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That's enormous. So why did police take him to hospital?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: They said his health was deteriorating. A High Court ordered it based on doctors' advice. The activist began his hunger strike on June twenty-eighth.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Is he stopping the strike now that he's hospitalized?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: His wife says he plans to continue it, even from the hospital.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw something about a banner at the World Cup semi-final. What happened?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: After Argentina beat England 2-1 in Atlanta on Wednesday, some Argentine players held up a banner that said 'Las Malvinas son Argentinas' — that's Argentina's name for the Falkland Islands, claiming them as their territory.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: And the White House actually weighed in on this?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes. A White House official said they believe in first amendment rights in the United States, and that Argentina's players have the opportunity to make statements while they're here.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: How did Britain respond?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Keir Starmer supported calls for FIFA to investigate the incident on Friday. He also appeared to back Spain ahead of the World Cup final against Argentina.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Why does this matter beyond just a sports moment?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: The White House backing Argentina on this could prove awkward for Andy Burnham, who's set to become British prime minister on Monday. It touches on a longstanding territorial dispute between Britain and Argentina.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So it's really about more than football.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Exactly. It's where sports, politics, and international relations all collide.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So this conflict between the US and Iran is still going?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes. The US launched a seventh consecutive night of strikes on Iran on Friday night.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What's the stated aim of these strikes?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: US Central Command said they're designed to continue degrading Iranian military capabilities.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Where exactly did these strikes happen?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Iranian media reported explosions or strikes in the cities of Sirik, Ahvaz, and Yazd.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: And has Iran responded?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes. On Saturday, Iran said it targeted a US air base in Bahrain and an intelligence data center there.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Why does this matter so much right now?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: This is the seventh day of fighting, and it's undermining an interim deal that was meant to keep the Strait of Hormuz open and allow space for negotiations.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So the situation is getting worse, not better.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: That's right. The fighting is escalating despite efforts to reach a permanent truce.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw something about lettuce being pulled from stores. What's going on?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Taylor Farms is voluntarily removing iceberg lettuce from the U.S. market after a cyclosporiasis outbreak.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What is cyclosporiasis?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It's an intestinal illness caused by a parasite. The FDA traced the outbreak to lettuce from central Mexico.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: How much lettuce are we talking about here?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Taylor Farms is pulling all iceberg lettuce from that region, even though the FDA identified one specific farm representing less than one percent of U.S. supply.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That seems like a lot more than necessary.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: They're being cautious. Taylor Farms is huge—they sell over seven billion dollars in produce yearly and make two out of every five salad kits in grocery stores.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Have people gotten sick from other places, not just Taco Bell?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes, not all reported cases have been linked to Taco Bell. The outbreak appears broader than one restaurant chain.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So Pebble smartwatches are back?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes. The founder, Eric Migicovsky, revived the brand early last year after the original company shut down in 2016.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That's cool. But I'm seeing something about a really short warranty?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Right. The new watches come with just a 30-day warranty, which has some people concerned.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Thirty days? That seems... really short.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It is. The Pebble Time 2's battery lasts about that long on a charge, so the warranty could expire before you even need to plug it in.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What's Migicovsky saying about this?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: He says it's about trust. He wants buyers to know what they're signing up for and to trust that Pebble will make things right if issues come up.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Are people actually buying them despite the short coverage?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Some are. Orders are going out, though the warranty question is definitely on people's minds.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw Russia attacked Kyiv overnight. What happened?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Ukraine says Russia fired its largest number of ballistic missiles at Kyiv since the war began. The attack lasted hours and hit early Sunday morning.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: How bad was the damage?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: One person was killed and at least sixteen were wounded. Fires broke out across the city, damaging homes, offices, industrial sites, and a dormitory.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: This is the fifth year of the war. Why is this attack significant now?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Ukraine is facing a shortage of Patriot air defence missiles, which are its best tool for stopping ballistic missiles. That makes these almost daily Russian attacks harder to defend against.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What's Ukraine saying about it?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Their foreign minister called it a brutal terrorist attack on the capital and said it demands a firm response.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 日曜日の夜明け前、キーウに大量の弾道ミサイルが降り注ぎました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ウクライナ外相によると、開戦以来で最も多い数が一度に撃ち込まれました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: この攻撃で1人が亡くなり、少なくとも16人が負傷しています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 住宅や事務所、工場、寮など、街の各所で火災が起きました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 外相のシビハ氏は、首都への残虐なテロ行為だと強く非難しました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ウクライナは今、戦争5年目に入ってもほぼ毎日攻撃を受け続けています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 弾道ミサイルを迎撃できるパトリオットの在庫が不足しており、防衛の余地が狭まっています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 首都への圧力がさらに高まるなか、ウクライナにとって厳しい局面が続きます。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So Spain and Argentina are meeting in the World Cup final on Sunday?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: That's right. It's the reigning European champions against the defending world champions.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What makes this matchup interesting?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It's really two different styles. Spain have controlled games all tournament, keeping possession and pressing high. They've only conceded one goal.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: And Argentina plays differently?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: The article describes them as contrasting philosophies, yes. One key battle will be Spain's top scorer Mikel Oyarzabal against Argentina's tough centre-backs Romero and Martinez.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Any other matchups to watch?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Lamine Yamal on Spain's right wing against Argentina's left-back Tagliafico. Yamal's been recovering from injury but he's getting closer to full strength.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Should be a good one then.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Two very accomplished teams. Spain chasing their second World Cup, Argentina going for their fourth.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 日曜日、2026年ワールドカップの決勝戦が幕を開けます。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: スペインとアルゼンチンが、世界の頂点をかけて対峙します。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: アルゼンチンは4度目の優勝を、スペインは2度目の栄冠を狙っています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: スペインはここまで大会通じてわずか1失点という、堅固な守備を誇ります。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ボールを握り続け、高い位置で奪い返すスタイルが、その安定感を支えています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 大会5得点のオジャルサバルに対し、アルゼンチンはロメロとリサンドロ・マルティネスが立ちはだかります。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 右サイドではヤマルとタリアフィコの一対一が、試合の流れを決める場面になりそうです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ヤマルは負傷の影響で出遅れましたが、徐々に本来の鋭さを取り戻しています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 対照的な哲学を持つ両チームが、どう噛み合うかが見どころです。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I'm seeing reports about US and Iran exchanging strikes. What happened?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Two US soldiers were killed in Jordan on Friday, and another service member is still missing. The US says Iran's Revolutionary Guards were responsible for that attack.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: And the US responded?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes. US Central Command said it targeted the Revolutionary Guards unit responsible. Then Iran said it carried out drone attacks on two US bases in Kuwait.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So this is escalating between them?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It does appear to be ramping up. Both sides have been accused of striking critical infrastructure in recent days.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What did Iran say about all this?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Iran's Supreme Leader released a statement on Saturday saying America's repeated breaches of agreements show that a US president's signature is worthless and without credibility.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That sounds pretty serious.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It is. The tension between the two countries is clearly growing, and the situation remains very fluid.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ヨルダンの米軍基地で、アメリカ兵2名が攻撃を受けて死亡しました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 米中央軍はイラン革命防衛隊の関与を断定し、関連施設への報復攻撃に踏み切りました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: なお、もう1名の行方はまだ確認されていません。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: これを受けてイランは。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: クウェートにある米軍のキャンプ・ウダイリとアリ・アルサレム空軍基地に自爆型ドローンを飛ばしたと主張しました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 米国とイランの間では、ここ数日で攻撃の応酬が急速にエスカレートしています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 重要インフラへの攻撃も双方から指摘されており、緊張は一段と高まっています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 土曜日の夜、イランのハメネイ最高指導者は書面で声明を出しました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: その中で、米国の度重なる合意違反が「米大統領の署名には信頼性がない」という事実を示したと述べました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 中東の情勢は、当面は予断を許さない状況が続きそうです。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So, facial recognition for your front door? That sounds very sci-fi.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It is. A tech writer tested four smart locks that unlock just by looking at them, and she was actually impressed.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Really? I thought those would be gimmicky or unreliable.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: That was the surprise. She found that if you want hands-free unlocking without relying on a device, facial recognition is worth considering.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What's the appeal of not using a device?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Well, you don't need to pull out your phone or wear a smartwatch. You just walk up, and the door recognizes you and unlocks.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Is facial recognition the best option for hands-free?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: She says hands-free with something called UWB is actually better, but facial recognition still gives you that futuristic, convenient experience.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So it's a real option now, not just a novelty.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Exactly. It's becoming a legitimate choice for people who want speed and convenience at their front door.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 玄関のドアが、顔を向けるだけで開く時代になってきました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 顔認証を使ったスマートロックが、実用的な選択肢として注目されています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 4つの製品を実際に試したところ、どれも日常的に使えるレベルの精度がありました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 鍵もスマートフォンも取り出さずに解錠できる体験は、思った以上に快適です。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: UWBを使ったハンズフリー解錠と比べると、速度や精度では一歩譲る部分もあります。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ただ、スマートフォンへの依存をなくしたい場合には、顔認証の方が向いています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: デバイスを持ち歩かなくても、素早く手軽に帰宅できる点が大きな魅力です。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 玄関まわりの体験を静かに変えてくれる、そんな技術が手の届くところに来ています。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So this is about reading setups? The headline mentions apps and gadgets for readers.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes, exactly. A couple of weeks ago, someone asked readers to share their reading setups — everything from gadgets and apps to bookstores, bookmark brands, and highlighter colors.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What kind of tools did people recommend?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: One standout is an app for writing across Apple devices — described as clever, useful, and still one of the best.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Anything else?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: There's also a new Mac-based photo editor that's been getting good reviews. It's fast, powerful, and aimed at replacing Adobe's tools, which keep getting more expensive.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So it's a mix of reading and creative tools?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It seems that way. The focus is on what readers find helpful in their setups, whether for reading or related creative work.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: Appleのデバイスをまたいで使える文章作成アプリが、いま改めて注目を集めています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: どのデバイスで開いても書き心地が変わらない点が、長く支持される理由のひとつです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 一方、Mac向けの写真編集アプリにも新しい動きがあります。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: Adobeのツールは近年、価格の上昇が続いていました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: その代替として設計されたこのアプリは、速度と機能の両面で高い評価を得ています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: また数週間前、読者のみなさんに読書環境を教えてほしいと呼びかけました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ガジェットやアプリだけでなく、しおりやマーカーの色まで、幅広い声が届きました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 道具や環境が整うと、読む時間の質がすこし変わってくるものです。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So we have a new prime minister this morning?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes. Andy Burnham met King Charles at Buckingham Palace and formally became prime minister.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That's the fourth PM in less than four years, right?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Exactly. And the sixth in a decade. It's been a turbulent period.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: How did Burnham become leader so quickly?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: No one else in the Labour party competed for the role, so he became leader without a contest.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Wasn't he the mayor of Greater Manchester?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: He was. He left parliament in 2017 for that job and only returned to parliament a month ago.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So he's been out of Westminster for years?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes, though he was an MP and cabinet minister before. He took over from Keir Starmer, who resigned to the King just before Burnham's appointment.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What happens now?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: He'll head to Downing Street to make a statement and announce his cabinet.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: イギリスでアンディ・バーナム氏が新しい首相に就きました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: バッキンガム宮殿でチャールズ国王と会い、正式に任命を受けています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: これで4年足らずのあいだに4人目の首相です。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: バーナム氏は労働党の党首になったばかりで、党内に対抗する人がいなかったため選挙なしで首相の座に就きました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 彼は以前、国会議員や閣僚を務めていましたが、2017年にグレーター・マンチェスター市長になるため議会を離れていました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 議会に戻ってきたのはわずか1か月前のことです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: スターマー前首相が国王に辞表を出した直後、バーナム氏が後を継ぎました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: これからダウニング街で声明を出し、新しい内閣を発表する予定です。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Spain just won the World Cup?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes, they beat Argentina one-nil in the final on Sunday. It's their second World Cup title.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: How are people celebrating?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Fans have been filling the streets with Spanish flags, setting off fireworks, celebrating into the early hours. It's happening in towns and cities across the country.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Who scored the winning goal?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Ferran Torres, in extra time.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I imagine a lot of people were rooting for Argentina too, because of Messi.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Absolutely. Many fans were wearing his number ten shirt during the match. He still has ties to Spain—he owns property in Castelldefels.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So it must have been bittersweet for some Spanish fans.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: In a way, yes. But for La Roja supporters, it's pure joy.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: スペインがワールドカップでアルゼンチンを1対0で破り、2度目の優勝を果たしました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 決勝点は延長戦、フェラン・トーレスが決めたものでした。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 試合後、カステルデフェルスをはじめ各地で赤と金の旗を手にした人々が街に繰り出しました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 花火が夜空を彩り、歓声は月曜の明け方まで続いたそうです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 興味深いのは、会場にアルゼンチンの10番を着た人が多くいたことです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: メッシの影響で、対戦相手を応援する人も少なくありませんでした。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 彼は今もこの町に専用グラウンド付きの邸宅を持っています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: スペインの勝利は、ある意味で地元の英雄への複雑な思いも映し出しているのかもしれません。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I'm seeing news about a ferry that sank off Guyana. What happened?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Late Saturday night, a passenger ferry called the MV Barima sank off the coast of Guyana. Officials are calling it one of the country's worst maritime disasters.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Where was this ferry going?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It left Georgetown, the capital, Saturday afternoon around 3:15, heading to Port Kaituma in the Essequibo region near the Venezuelan border.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: How did rescuers find out?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: A distress call came in shortly after 11 p.m. That triggered a big response—the coast guard, air corps, private aircraft, fishing boats, and civilian volunteers all joined the search.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Do we know what caused it?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Authorities have launched a criminal investigation. A government official said the captain and a crew member allegedly tested positive for marijuana, and the passenger manifest was inaccurate.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That sounds serious. Are there survivors?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes, there are survivors. One woman who regularly travels that route to sell clothing survived by sharing a lifejacket with another passenger.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 南米ガイアナの沖で、乗客を乗せたフェリーが土曜の夜に沈みました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 現地当局は、国内で起きた最悪級の海難事故だとしています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: フェリーは首都から石油が豊かな地域の港へ向かう途中でした。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 夜11時過ぎに救難信号が入り、軍や民間の船が出動しました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 船長と乗組員から大麻の陽性反応が出たとされ、乗客名簿にも誤りがあったため、刑事捜査が始まっています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 生存者の一人は、別の乗客と同じ救命胴衣につかまって助かりました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 捜索活動は今も続いています。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw something about Four Tet releasing music under a name that's basically impossible to say out loud?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes, Kieran Hebden, who makes music as Four Tet, has this side project where even the artist name is just a series of symbols and glyphs — totally unpronounceable.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Wait, so how do people even refer to it?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Most people are just calling the album Wingdings, like the old computer font. Hebden's kept this alias pretty quiet for years.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That's kind of playful. Is it hard to find?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Not at all. It's on Bandcamp and all the major streaming services — Apple Music, Spotify, Deezer, Qobuz.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So he's not trying to hide it anymore.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Seems like it. It's out there for anyone curious enough to track it down.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 電子音楽家のKieran Hebdenが、長年隠してきた別の名義で作品を発表していたことがわかりました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: その名義は記号だけで構成されていて、声に出して読むことができません。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: Four Tetという名前なら聞いたことがある人も多いでしょう。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 実は同じ人物が、誰にも読めない記号の羅列を名前にした別のプロジェクトを続けていたのです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: このアルバムはWingdingsと呼ばれ、BandcampやApple Music、Spotifyで聴けます。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 音楽家が複数の名義を使うこと自体は珍しくありませんが、ここまで徹底して隠すのは異例です。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 表現の幅を広げたいのか、それとも作品そのものだけを聴いてほしいのか、意図は明かされていません。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw something about a new Kodak film camera that's really cheap?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes, it's called the EC35. It's a 35mm film camera that costs just under thirty-five dollars.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Wow, that is cheap. Who's making it?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: A company called Reto Project. They previously released a Kodak-branded camera for ninety-nine dollars, and this one's even more affordable.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What's the idea behind it?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It's designed to be really simple—a point-and-shoot that requires almost no thought to use. Just pick it up and take pictures.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Does it at least look nice, or is it just super basic?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It has some nice touches. The lens covers are a slightly darker shade than the body, which gives a subtle contrast. Even the black and white versions have different colored accents.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So a bit more stylish than those old disposable cameras.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Exactly. It fits into this ongoing film photography revival, making it more accessible for people who want to try shooting film without spending much.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: フィルムで撮る楽しみが、また少し身近になりました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: Reto Projectが、コダックの名を冠した新しいカメラを出します。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: EC35という名前で、値段は35ドル弱です。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 35ミリのフィルムを使う、手のひらサイズのカメラです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 操作はほとんど考えなくていい作りで、レンズの蓋には軽い色の違いがあります。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 黒も白も、小さな色のアクセントがついていて、使い捨てよりは少し洒落ています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: フィルムを使う人が静かに増えている流れの中で、こうした選択肢が広がっています。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So Trump just put a fifty percent tariff on Canadian goods?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes, on a wide range of imports from Canada. He says it's retaliation for what he calls unequal treatment of US cars, dairy, and alcohol.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That's a huge tariff. How did Canada respond?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Prime Minister Mark Carney said Canada is ready to intensify trade talks with the US in the coming weeks.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Has this been building for a while?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It has. These tensions have been simmering since Trump returned to office in January and started a wide-ranging global tariff program.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Just to be clear, what exactly is a tariff?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It's a tax on imported goods. Companies bringing in foreign products pay it to the government.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: And why does this matter so much between these two countries?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It marks a major escalation in trade tensions between the North American neighbors. They're deeply connected economically.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: アメリカのトランプ大統領が、カナダからの輸入品に50%の関税をかけました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 理由は、自動車や乳製品、酒類の扱いが不公平だというものです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: これに対し、カナダのカーニー首相は、今後数週間で交渉を強める考えを示しています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 関税とは、海外から商品を運び込む企業が政府に納める税金のことです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: トランプ大統領は今年1月に就任して以来、世界各地に向けて関税を広げてきました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 今回の措置は、隣り合う二つの国の間で、貿易の対立が新たな段階に入ったことを意味します。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 朝の買い物や仕事に使う製品の値段にも、影響が出る可能性があります。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw something about protesters being called cockroaches in India. What's happening there?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Thousands of protesters tried to march on parliament in Delhi, demanding the education minister resign. Police used tear gas and canes to stop them.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Why are they called the cockroach protesters?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: The movement is called the Cockroach Janta Party. The name comes from a comment India's chief justice made during a court hearing, comparing unemployed young people to cockroaches.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That's pretty harsh. What are they actually asking for?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: They're a youth-led group seeking education and employment reforms. The protests grew after police forcibly hospitalized an activist named Sonam Wangchuk.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What happened with him?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: He was on a hunger strike supporting the movement. Police moved him to hospital on the twenty-first day. On Monday, he asked doctors to discharge him so he could join the march, but wasn't allowed.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: インドの首都デリーで、教育と雇用の改革を求める若者たちが国会に向かって行進しようとしました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 警察は催涙ガスと警棒を使い、数千人の群衆を強制的に解散させています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: この運動は「コックローチ・ジャンタ党」と名乗っています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 名前の由来は、最高裁の長官が法廷で失業中の若者を「ゴキブリ」に例えた発言です。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 週末には、活動家のソナム・ワンチュクがハンガーストライキの21日目に強制入院させられました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: これをきっかけに、運動への支持がさらに広がっています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ワンチュクは行進に加わるため退院を求めましたが、認められていません。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 若者の不満が、こうした形で表に出ている状況です。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw something about US troops being injured in the Middle East. What's happening?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: The Pentagon confirmed that nearly 100 service members have been injured over the last two weeks during escalating strikes involving Iran.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That's a lot. How serious are the injuries?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: According to Pentagon spokesperson Sean Parnell, most experienced minor concussions, and 96% have already returned to work.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So Iran is striking back at US facilities?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes. Tehran launched reprisals following ten consecutive nights of attacks. Kuwait, Bahrain, and Jordan all activated defenses to repel those strikes.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Why are we only hearing about this now?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Parnell was responding to a New York Times report suggesting the Pentagon withheld information about troop injuries from Iranian strikes.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Is this affecting anything beyond the region?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: The escalation has pushed oil prices higher in recent weeks, so it's having broader economic impact.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ペンタゴンが、この2週間で米軍兵士およそ100人が負傷していたと発表しました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: イランが連日続けている攻撃への報復として、クウェートやバーレーン、ヨルダンといった米国の同盟国にも攻撃が及んでいます。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 各国は防衛システムを作動させて迎撃にあたりました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 負傷者の大半は軽い脳震盪で、96%はすでに任務に戻っているとペンタゴンは説明しています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: この発表は、負傷の情報が公にされていなかったという報道を受けたものです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 中東での緊張が続く中、原油の価格も上がり始めています。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So Ford is starting over with electric trucks?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: In a way, yes. They're ending production of the F-150 Lightning and shifting to a completely new approach with what they're calling a Universal Electric Vehicle Platform.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What went wrong with the Lightning?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Ford says it didn't capture the market the way they hoped. So now they're focusing on something different — efficiency and profitability.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: And this new truck is going to be cheaper?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Much cheaper. They're targeting a thirty thousand dollar price point, which is significantly lower than the Lightning.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: When can people actually buy one?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It'll be revealed in 2027. Ford plans to build it at their Kentucky assembly plant, along with a whole family of low-cost electric vehicles.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: How small are we talking here?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Ford suggests it could be smaller than their Maverick, which is already their compact truck. So this is really about rethinking what an electric truck can be.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: フォードが、3万ドルで買える電動トラックを作ります。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 同社の人気モデルFシリーズの電動版は、期待ほど売れませんでした。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: そこで設計を一から見直し、新しい共通の電動基盤を使うことにしたのです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: この新しいトラックは、効率と利益の両立を最優先に開発されています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 2027年に姿を見せる予定で、ケンタッキーの工場では低価格の電動車を次々と作る計画です。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: サイズは既存のマーベリックより小さくなるかもしれません。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 手の届く価格の電動トラックが、選択肢として現実味を帯びてきました。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw something about a big game bundle deal. What's going on?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Humble is selling what they're calling the 2K Megahits 2026 Bundle — 15 Steam games for $15.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Fifteen dollars for fifteen games? What's in it?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It's a collection of 2K's biggest games from the 2010s. Think BioShock Infinite, Borderlands 3, XCOM: Enemy Unknown, and Risk of Rain 2.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Those are pretty well-known titles. How long is this available?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Until August 7th. Though the bundle page says keys for some games are already running low.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So if someone's interested, they shouldn't wait too long.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Exactly. All the games work on Windows, and some also support MacOS and SteamOS.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: PCゲームの配信サイトHumbleで、15本のゲームを15ドルで買えるバンドルが販売されています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 2Kという会社が2010年代に出した代表作が揃っています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: BioShock InfiniteやBorderlands 3。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: XCOM: Enemy Unknownといった人気作が含まれています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: すべてWindowsで遊べて、一部はMacOSやSteamOSにも対応しています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 販売は8月7日までですが、一部のタイトルはすでにキーの在庫が少なくなっているようです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: まとめ買いでゲームを増やしたい人には、手頃な機会かもしれません。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So the US just signed a nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes, it's described as a peaceful nuclear cooperation agreement. It allows Saudi Arabia to develop a civilian nuclear programme.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What does that actually mean for the US?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It could be worth billions of dollars. American companies would get access to help build facilities, including ones that produce enriched uranium for power plants.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Wait, enriched uranium... isn't that also used for weapons?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It can be. The US says the deal upholds high standards of nuclear safety and nonproliferation, and strengthens American competitive edge in civil nuclear technology.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Are people worried about that weapons possibility?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Some are. Nuclear experts and former officials in Israel have voiced concern the plan could ultimately lead to Saudi Arabia being able to develop a nuclear weapon.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So there's a tension between the business opportunity and the security risk.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Exactly. The US is emphasizing regional security and nonproliferation standards, but the concern remains.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: アメリカとサウジアラビアが、サウジの民間向け原子力開発を認める協定に署名しました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: この協定で、アメリカ企業がサウジ国内のウラン濃縮施設の建設に関わる道が開かれます。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ウラン濃縮は発電所の燃料として使われますが、核兵器の材料にもなり得る技術です。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: アメリカ政府は、この協定が核の安全と不拡散の高い基準を守りながら、自国の民間原子力技術の競争力を高めると説明しています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 一方で、イスラエルの専門家や元政府関係者からは懸念の声が上がっています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 将来サウジが核兵器を持つ可能性につながるのではないか、という見方です。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 協定の規模は数十億ドルに達するとみられ、アメリカ企業にとって大きな商機となります。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 中東の安全保障の構図が、静かに変わり始めているのかもしれません。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw that Ukraine's president just fired his top military commander. What's happening there?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: President Zelenskyy removed the country's army chief from his position. This comes just days after he also dismissed the defense minister.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So both the top military leaders are out in less than a week?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes. These were the two top figures leading Ukraine's war effort against Russia.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That seems like a pretty big deal during an ongoing war.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It is. This is being called one of the most significant military shakeups in the past four years of the war.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Do we know why he made these changes now?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: The reporting doesn't specify the reasons behind the dismissals. What we know is the timing and that protests are ongoing in the country.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That's a lot of uncertainty at a critical moment.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It is. Leadership changes like this during wartime always raise questions about strategy and direction going forward.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ウクライナのゼレンスキー大統領が、軍の総司令官を解任しました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: その数日前には国防相も更迭されていて、1週間足らずで軍の最高幹部2人が職を失ったことになります。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ロシアとの戦争が始まって4年になりますが、これほど大きな軍の指導部の入れ替えは初めてです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 戦時下で軍を率いる立場の人事が短期間に動くのは、異例のことです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 背景には国内での抗議活動も続いていて、政権への圧力が高まっている様子がうかがえます。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 今後、新しい指導部がどのように戦況に向き合うのか、注目されます。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: There's a troubling case out of Hong Kong involving a British woman. What happened?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: A 26-year-old woman from south-east London, Isabel Rose, has been sentenced to six years in prison there.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What was she convicted of?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Blackmail and perverting the course of public justice. She had accused a British banker of rape after travelling to Hong Kong to visit him.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So the court didn't believe her accusation?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: The judge called the allegation completely fabricated. She said Rose had not shown any remorse.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: How did they know each other?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: They'd met while travelling in south-east Asia — she was on holiday in Thailand. She travelled to Hong Kong in January 2024 to visit him.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What led to the blackmail charge?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: The judge said Rose made escalating demands, which amounted to blackmail.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 香港で、英国人女性が懲役6年の判決を受けました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 女性は警察にレイプ被害を訴えていましたが、裁判所は恐喝と司法妨害の罪を認定しました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 26歳のイザベル・ローズは、タイ旅行中に知り合った英国人銀行家を訪ねて香港へ渡っていました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 裁判官は、彼女の訴えを完全な虚偽と判断し、金銭要求が段階的に高まった点を恐喝と認めました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ローズは一切の反省を示さなかったと、判決の場で指摘されています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 虚偽の申告が重い刑罰につながった事例として、記録に残ることになります。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw something about iPads being on sale. What's the deal?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: There's a model with a terabyte of storage and optional 5G that's marked down to seven forty-nine. That's five hundred dollars off the suggested retail price.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Five hundred off? That's a big discount. Are there other tech deals happening right now?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes, a few. Sony is selling refurbished PlayStation 5 Slim consoles for four ninety-nine, which is a hundred dollars less than usual.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Refurbished though—does that mean there's something wrong with them?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: They may have minor cosmetic imperfections, but Sony tests and certifies them to perform just as well as new consoles.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Got it. What else is discounted?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Apple AirTags are on sale—a four pack of the second generation is eighty-nine ninety-nine at Amazon, matching their lowest price ever. And there's a Razer gaming mouse for about thirty dollars off.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: Appleの大容量iPadが、いま通常より大幅に安く買えるようになっています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 1テラバイトの保存容量と5G通信に対応したモデルが、定価から500ドル引きの749ドルです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ソニーは整備済みのプレイステーション5スリムを499ドルで出していて、これは通常より100ドル安い価格です。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 見た目に小さな傷があることもありますが、ソニーが検査して性能を保証しています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: アップルのエアタグ第2世代の4個セットは、アマゾンで89.99ドルに下がっていて、これまでで最も安い水準です。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: レイザーのワイヤレスゲーミングマウス、ヴァイパーV3プロも、通常より30ドルほど安い99.99ドルで手に入ります。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 値上げが続くなかで、こうした割引は買い替えを考えている人にとって貴重な機会になりそうです。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So Tesla's doing better than it was?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes, revenues are bouncing back after two tough years of falling sales and weakening demand. They just passed a hundred billion dollars in revenue over the last twelve months for the first time.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That sounds good. But the headline says profits are still weak?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Right. They're actually in negative free cash flow right now because they're spending heavily on big projects like AI infrastructure and robotics.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So they're making more money but spending even more?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Exactly. They've also been working on reducing inventory, which helps their balance sheet. And they're moving ahead with production plans for the Cybercab in Texas and the Semi truck in Nevada later this year.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Is Tesla still mainly a car company despite all these other projects?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes, despite everything else, Tesla remains a car company at its core.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: Teslaの売上が、ここ2年ほどの落ち込みから持ち直してきました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 直近12か月の合計で、初めて1000億ドルを超えたそうです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ただ、利益の面ではまだ弱さが残っています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 背景には、AIやロボット開発への大きな投資があります。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: そのため、自由に使える現金の流れはマイナスが続いています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: テキサスやネバダの工場では、新しい車の生産が進んでいるとのことです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 売上の回復は見えても、収益力が戻るかは今後の課題になりそうです。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw the U.S. just made a nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia. What's this about?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It's an agreement for U.S. companies to build and run nuclear energy plants in Saudi Arabia. The program is expected to last decades and be worth billions of dollars.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Wait, Saudi Arabia has all that oil. Why do they need nuclear power?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: They're trying to pivot to other energy sources. Nuclear power would let them use less petroleum at home and sell more oil abroad.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So why is Israel upset about this?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: There's concern in Israel that this shows a growing gap between their priorities and the Trump administration's approach to the region, especially on security issues.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Is the timing significant here?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes. This is happening while the U.S. and Iran have been trading strikes in the Persian Gulf, which adds to the tension.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That does seem like a complicated moment for this kind of deal.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It is. Israel sees itself as a major American ally, so any shift in how the U.S. engages with Saudi Arabia gets close attention there.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: There's a lot happening in the Gulf region right now. Can you walk me through what's going on?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes. The US has been conducting strikes against Iran for twelve consecutive nights now, and early Thursday that continued. Iran has been responding by striking targets across the Gulf.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Where specifically has Iran been targeting?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Particularly in Kuwait and Jordan, according to reports. And Iran's Revolutionary Guard also said a tanker caught fire in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday after an explosion.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That's a critical shipping route. What about the Houthis—are they involved too?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: They are. The Houthis claimed responsibility for a major operation against two Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea—vessels called the Encelia and the Layla.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So we're seeing attacks on shipping from multiple directions.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Exactly. It's a tense situation with strikes and retaliation happening across several key waterways and countries in the region.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw something about protests in India called 'cockroach' protests. What's happening there?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: There's been a youth-led protest movement in Delhi over examination paper leaks. Students are upset because officials allegedly leaked exam papers, which undermines the fairness of the system.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: And Modi just responded to this?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes, in his first public remarks on the movement. He posted that nothing is more important than the welfare and future of young people, and promised those who harm their future won't be spared.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What's he actually doing about it?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: He's vowed to set up fast-track courts to speed up punishment for officials accused of leaking the papers. It seems the government may be softening its stance after facing this challenge.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Is this just happening in Delhi?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: No, young people across India have joined in. There've been three consecutive days of demonstrations in Mumbai, plus solidarity protests in Chennai, Indore, Hyderabad, and Patiala.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Why is this such a big deal for Modi's government?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It's being described as one of the biggest challenges to his government. The scale and persistence of the youth movement has really put pressure on leadership to respond.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw something about iPads being cheaper right now. What's the deal?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: There's a model with a terabyte of storage and optional 5G that's marked down to seven forty-nine. That's five hundred dollars off the suggested retail price.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Five hundred off? That's a big discount. Are there other tech deals happening too?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yeah, a few. Sony is selling refurbished PlayStation 5 Slim consoles for four ninety-nine, which is a hundred dollars less than usual.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Refurbished though—does that mean they're not as good?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: They may have minor cosmetic imperfections, but Sony tests and certifies them to perform just as well as new consoles.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Got it. Anything else on sale?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Apple AirTags are discounted. A four-pack of the second generation is eighty-nine ninety-nine at Amazon—that matches their lowest price ever. And there's a Razer gaming mouse for about thirty dollars off.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So Tesla's revenues are improving. Does that mean the company's doing well again?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It's a mixed picture. Yes, revenues are bouncing back after two tough years of falling sales and weakening demand. They just crossed a hundred billion dollars over the past twelve months for the first time.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That sounds pretty good. What's the problem then?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Profits are still weak, and the company's actually in negative free cash flow right now. They're spending heavily on things like AI infrastructure and robotics.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Right, but Tesla's more than just cars now, isn't it?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Despite all those other projects, Tesla remains fundamentally a car company. They did manage to shrink their inventory though, which helps their balance sheet.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Are they making progress on those newer vehicles?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: They say Cybercab production is happening at their Texas factory, and Semi production at the Nevada facility is still on track for later this year.
